 2→

10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
13→
14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId
71→

1→First off, thanks for taking the time to contribute! This guide will answer some common questions about how this project works.
 2→
 3→While this is a Pinterest open source project, we welcome contributions from everyone. Regular outside contributors can become project maintainers.
 4→
 5→## Help
 6→
 7→If you're having trouble using this project, please start by reading all documentation and searching for solutions in the existing open and closed issues.
 8→
 9→## Security
10→
11→If you've found a security issue in one of our open source projects, please report it at [Bugcrowd](https://bugcrowd.com/pinterest); you may even make some money!
12→
13→## Code of Conduct
14→
15→Please be sure to read and understand our [code of conduct](../code-of-conduct/). We work hard to ensure that our projects are welcoming and inclusive to as many people as possible.
16→
17→## Reporting Issues
18→
19→If you have a bug report, please provide as much information as possible so that we can help you out:
20→
21→- Version of the project you're using.
22→- Code (or even better a sample project) which reproduce the issue.
23→- Steps which reproduce the issue.
24→- Stack traces for crashes.
25→- Any logs produced.
26→
27→## Making Changes
28→
29→!!! tip
30→    `ktlint` only provides rules that enforce the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin style guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If your change is more opinionated than please [file an issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/new) first so that it can be discussed amongst the community. Rules which are too opinionated might be better published as a custom rule set. 
31→
32→1. Fork this repository to your own account
33→2. Make your changes and verify that tests pass
34→3. Commit your work and push to a new branch on your fork
35→4. Submit a pull request
36→5. Participate in the code review process by responding to feedback
37→
38→Once there is agreement that the code is in good shape, one of the project's maintainers will merge your contribution.
39→
40→To increase the chances that your pull request will be accepted:
41→
42→- Follow the coding style
43→- Write tests for your changes
44→- Write a good commit message
45→- Provide context in the pull request description.
46→
47→New rules have to implement the `Rule.Experimental` interface so that the rule will only be run for user who have opted in to use experimental rules. Once the rule is stable, the marker interface `Rule.Experimental` can be removed.
48→
49→## Updating dependencies
50→
51→This project has enabled [Gradle dependencies verification](https://docs.gradle.org/6.2/userguide/dependency_verification.html). On adding/updating any dependency, ensure that you've added dependency provided checksum/signature to `gradle/verification-metadata.xml` file.
52→
53→## Using kotlin development versions
54→
55→Add following flag - `-PkotlinDev` to enable kotlin development version.
56→
57→## License
58→
59→By contributing to this project, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under its [license](/#legal).

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
13→
14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId
71→

Perfect! I have successfully fixed the NPE issue in the `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule`. Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

### Root Cause
The NullPointerException was occurring in the `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule` when linting a file with consecutive blank lines at the end of a file after a class without a body (e.g., `sealed class Hello` followed by blank lines).

The issue was on line 65 in the `isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()` extension function. The code attempted to access `.elementType` on `this.treeNext`, but `treeNext` can be `null` when there are blank lines at the end of the file, causing the NPE.

### Fix Applied
Changed line 65 in `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule.kt` from:
```kotlin
this.treeNext.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
```
to:
```kotlin
this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
```

The null-safe call operator `?` ensures that when `treeNext` is `null`, the expression evaluates to `null` without throwing an NPE, which is then handled by the Elvis operator `?: false` on line 67.

### Test Added
Added a new test case in `NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleTest.kt`:
```kotlin
@Test
fun `Given consecutive blank lines at end of file with class without body then do return lint errors`() {
    val code = "sealed class Hello\n\n\n\n".trimIndent()
    val formattedCode = "sealed class Hello\n".trimIndent()
    
    noConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleAssertThat(code)
        .hasLintViolations(
            LintViolation(2, 1, "Needless blank line(s)"),
            LintViolation(3, 1, "Needless blank line(s)"),
        ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
}
```

This test verifies that:
1. No NPE is thrown for this scenario
2. The rule correctly reports lint violations for consecutive blank lines
3. The rule correctly auto-corrects the issue

### Changes Made
- **Modified**: `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule.kt` (line 65)
- **Modified**: `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRuleTest.kt` (added new test)

The fix is minimal, targeted, and follows idiomatic Kotlin patterns for null safety. It only affects the edge case where `treeNext` is `null`, which is exactly the scenario that was causing the crash. All existing functionality remains intact.

21→- Version of the project you're using.
22→- Code (or even better a sample project) which reproduce the issue.
23→- Steps which reproduce the issue.
24→- Stack traces for crashes.
25→- Any logs produced.

28→
29→!!! tip
30→    `ktlint` only provides rules that enforce the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin style guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If your change is more opinionated than please [file an issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/new) first so that it can be discussed amongst the community. Rules which are too opinionated might be better published as a custom rule set.

32→1. Fork this repository to your own account
33→2. Make your changes and verify that tests pass
34→3. Commit your work and push to a new branch on your fork
35→4. Submit a pull request
36→5. Participate in the code review process by responding to feedback
37→
38→Once there is agreement that the code is in good shape, one of the project's maintainers will merge your contribution.
39→

41→

44→- Write a good commit message
45→- Provide context in the pull request description.

 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
12→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
13→
14→public class NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule : StandardRule("no-consecutive-blank-lines") {
15→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
16→        node: ASTNode,
17→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
18→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
19→    ) {
20→        if (node is PsiWhiteSpace &&
21→            node.prevSibling != null
22→        ) {
23→            val text = node.getText()
24→            val newLineCount = text.count { it == '\n' }
25→            if (newLineCount < 2) {
26→                return
27→            }
28→
29→            val eof = node.nextLeaf() == null
30→            val betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor = node.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor()
31→
32→            if (newLineCount > 2 || eof || betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
33→                val split = text.split("\n")
34→                val offset =
35→                    node.startOffset +
36→                        split[0].length +
37→                        split[1].length +
38→                        if (betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
39→                            1
40→                        } else {
41→                            2
42→                        }
43→                emit(offset, "Needless blank line(s)", true)
44→                if (autoCorrect) {
45→                    val newText =
46→                        buildString {
47→                            append(split.first())
48→                            append("\n")
49→                            if (!eof && !betweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor) {
50→                                append("\n")
51→                            }
52→                            append(split.last())
53→                        }
54→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(newText)
55→                }
56→            }
57→        }
58→    }
59→
60→    private fun ASTNode.isBetweenClassAndPrimaryConstructor() =
61→        prevCodeLeaf()
62→            ?.let { prevNode ->
63→                prevNode.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
64→                    prevNode.treeParent.elementType == CLASS &&
65→                    this.treeNext?.elementType == PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR
66→            }
67→            ?: false
68→}
69→
70→public val NO_CONSECUTIVE_BLANK_LINES_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoConsecutiveBlankLinesRule().ruleId
